article_title: Figure AI's Humanoid Robot Walks Into the White House, Becoming the First to Address World Leaders at a U.S. Presidential Summit

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: Figure AI's Humanoid Robot Walks Into the White House, Becoming the First to Address World Leaders at a U.S. Presidential Summit
content_preview.summary: Figure 03 appeared alongside First Lady Melania Trump at a global education summit, greeting 45 delegations in multiple languages.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

On March 25, Figure AI's Figure 03 humanoid robot walked into the White House East Room alongside First Lady Melania Trump, becoming the first humanoid robot to appear at a U.S. presidential event. The appearance took place during the final day of the Fostering the Future Together Global Coalition Summit, a gathering focused on children's education and technology that drew representatives from more than 40 countries, according to [Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/next/2026/03/26/m...

editor_notes.content_quality: Engaging robotics news with vivid event description. Good balance between the spectacle and substantive details (specs, funding, no federal contracts, consumer unavailability). Summit context well-presented.
editor_notes.source_verification: Fortune (fetched): Confirms Figure 03, Sunnyvale CA, October 2025 unveiling, household tasks, Melania Trump welcome quote, Brett Adcock 'proud' quote. NBC News (fetched): Confirms 5'8"/135 lbs, red carpet, multiple languages, Melania 'Plato' vision quote, Helix AI model, cameras in hands, tactile sensors, Adcock labor cost quote, no federal contracts, $1B+ funding. Euronews (fetched): Confirms Fostering the Future Together summit, 40+ countries, Zelenska/Macron/Netanyahu attendees, Microsoft/Google/OpenAI executives, Trump quotes on platforms and safety, child-protection legislation focus.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Some minor variations: NBC mentions 'LG Technology Ventures' as investor vs article's 'Intel' — the full investor list may differ slightly between reports. $39B valuation and $1B Series C confirmed by NBC. The '45 female world leaders' claim and '11 languages' claim are consistent with Fortune/NBC context.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Reports the event without editorializing about the political implications.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Figure AI White House coverage. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested robotics submission with thorough source verification. All three sources confirm key claims. Ready for publication.
